step_by_step_thinking:
Based on the prior discussion, I would concur with the initial assessment that Option C represents a valid drawback associated with utilizing a grid system in radiographic procedures. One supplementary rationale reinforcing this perspective pertains to how improperly positioned grids could lead to inconsistent absorption rates across different regions of the imaging area, thereby generating unwanted variations in image quality known as 'radiation fog.' Such inconsistencies pose challenges for diagnostic interpretation and necessitate meticulous calibration practices to mitigate their occurrence.
